steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: $r$ identical objects have total mass $t$ kg, so each object has mass $\frac{t}{r}$ kg.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Total mass of $132r$ such objects: $132r \times \frac{t}{r} = 132t$ kg. The $r$ cancels.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: As a final check, compare the result from the work to the answer choices. The value, equation, or condition found above matches $132t$, and the other choices fail the same setup or substitution. Therefore D is correct.

choiceEliminations:
• Choice A — $132 - t$: Subtracting $t$ from 132 has no physical meaning for scaling mass. ✗
  • Choice B — $132 + t$: Adding $t$ to 132 does not reflect proportional scaling. ✗
  • Choice C — $\frac{t}{132}$: This would give the mass of $\frac{r}{132}$ objects, the opposite of what is needed. ✗
  • Choice D — $132t$: Multiplying $132r$ objects by $\frac{t}{r}$ each correctly gives $132t$. ✓
That is the correct answer.
